# GitHub Copilot instructions

These instructions are for GitHub Copilot when generating code in this repository. It covers the project overview, repository structure, CI/CD pipeline flow, coding conventions, and specific rules to follow when modifying files or generating code.

## AI instruction files

This repository maintains three AI instruction files that must always be kept in sync:

| File | Read by |
| --- | --- |
| `AGENTS.md` | OpenAI Codex and autonomous agents |
| `CLAUDE.md` | Claude (Anthropic) |
| `.github/copilot-instructions.md` | GitHub Copilot |

**Sync rule:** whenever you modify any of these files, you must apply the same change to the other two.

**Learning rule:** whenever you discover or correct something important about how this repository works ‚Äî a new convention, a pattern that caused issues, a rule clarification ‚Äî add it to all three files immediately so future AI sessions always have accurate context.

---

## Project overview

`actions-templates` is a centralized library of reusable GitHub Actions workflows
and composite actions maintained by Francis J. Garc√≠a (@francisjgarcia).

Consumer repositories call these templates from their own `.github/workflows/cicd.yml`
instead of duplicating pipeline logic. This centralizes all CI/CD logic so changes
propagate to all consumers at once.

This repo does **not** contain application code. It contains only:
- Reusable workflows (`wf-*.yml`)
- Composite actions (`actions/*/action.yml`)
- Documentation and examples

---

## Repository structure

```
actions-templates/
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ .github/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ISSUE_TEMPLATE/        # Issue forms (structured YAML)
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ workflows/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ cicd.yml           # This repo's own pipeline (uses ./ local refs)
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ lint.yml           # Calls wf-lint.yml with ./
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ pr-validation.yml  # Calls wf-pr-validation.yml with ./
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ codeql.yml         # CodeQL security analysis
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-setup.yml       # Reusable: semver + metadata
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-build.yml       # Reusable: docker build + artifact
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-tests.yml       # Reusable: flake8, pytest, custom
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-scan.yml        # Reusable: Anchore CVE scan + SARIF
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-push.yml        # Reusable: ghcr.io push
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-release.yml     # Reusable: CHANGELOG + GitHub release
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-deploy.yml      # Reusable: multi-container docker deploy
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-lint.yml        # Reusable: actionlint + yamllint
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-pr-validation.yml # Reusable: conventional commit PR title check
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ CODEOWNERS
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ copilot-instructions.md  # Instructions for GitHub Copilot
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ FUNDING.yml
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ dependabot.yml
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ release.yml            # GitHub release notes grouping by labels
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ actions/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ calculate-tag/
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ action.yml         # Composite: semver bump from conventional commits
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ docs/
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ usage/
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ python.md
‚îÇ       ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ nodejs.md
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ go.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ examples/
‚îÇ   ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ single-container/
‚îÇ   ‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ cicd.yml           # Ready-to-use example (can be modified)
‚îÇ   ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ multi-container/
‚îÇ       ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ cicd.yml           # Ready-to-use example (can be modified)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ AGENTS.md                  # Instructions for autonomous AI agents
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ ARCHITECTURE.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ AUTHORS
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ CHANGELOG.md               # Auto-generated by wf-release.yml ‚Äî do not edit manually
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ CLAUDE.md                  # Instructions for Claude AI assistant
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ CONTRIBUTING.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ CONVENTIONS.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ GOVERNANCE.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ LICENSE
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ README.md
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ SECURITY.md
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ SUPPORT.md
```

---

## CI/CD pipeline flow

```
push to main
    ‚îÇ
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-setup      ‚Üí calculate-tag (conventional commits) + repo metadata
    ‚îÇ                   outputs: tag_version, bump_type, changelog_entries, author_*, etc.
    ‚îÇ                   if bump_type=none ‚Üí all downstream jobs are skipped
    ‚îÇ
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-build      ‚Üí docker buildx + OCI labels + upload artifact (tar, 1 day retention)
    ‚îÇ
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-tests      ‚Üí runs inside built image (flake8, pytest, or custom command)
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-scan       ‚Üí Anchore CVE scan ‚Üí SARIF uploaded to Security tab
    ‚îÇ
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-push       ‚Üí ghcr.io push (semver tag + latest)
    ‚îÇ
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-release    ‚Üí CHANGELOG.md update + commit via GitHub App + GitHub release
    ‚îÇ                   release body: AI summary + grouped commit changelog
    ‚îÇ
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ wf-deploy     ‚Üí multi-container deploy via JSON array input
                        secrets passed via APP_ENV_VARS (JSON secret)

pull request to main
    ‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ lint          ‚Üí actionlint + yamllint
    ‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ pr-validation ‚Üí conventional commit format check on PR title
```

- `wf-setup` outputs `tag_version` ‚Äî if empty, ALL downstream jobs skip automatically
- `wf-build` uploads a docker image `.tar` artifact (retention: 1 day)
- `wf-tests` and `wf-scan` both download that artifact independently
- `wf-push` runs only after both tests and scan pass
- `wf-release` commits `CHANGELOG.md` via GitHub App token (bypasses branch protection)
- `wf-deploy` receives container config as JSON array + secrets as `APP_ENV_VARS` JSON secret

---

## Conventional commits

Every commit and PR title must follow this format:

```
<type>[optional scope]: <description>
```

Valid types: `feat`, `fix`, `perf`, `refactor`, `docs`, `chore`, `ci`, `test`, `style`, `build`

| Type | Semver bump | When to use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `feat!` or `BREAKING CHANGE:` | MAJOR | Breaking change in workflow inputs/outputs |
| `feat` | MINOR | New workflow, action, or input |
| `fix` | PATCH | Bug fix in existing workflow or action |
| `perf` | PATCH | Performance improvement |
| `refactor` | PATCH | Code refactor without behavior change |
| `docs` | none | Documentation only |
| `chore` | none | Maintenance |
| `ci` | none | CI/CD changes |
| `test` | none | Tests |

The highest bump in a set of commits wins. If a PR contains `fix:` and `feat:`, the result is MINOR.

Breaking changes: append `!` after type ‚Üí `feat!: rename input version`

Examples:
```
feat(wf-deploy): add dns input for container configuration
fix(calculate-tag): handle repos with no previous tags correctly
docs: update nodejs usage guide with test_command example
chore: update anchore/scan-action to v3.4.0
```

---

## File modification guidelines

| File / Directory | May modify | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `.github/workflows/wf-*.yml` | ‚úÖ Yes | Core reusable workflows |
| `actions/*/action.yml` | ‚úÖ Yes | Composite actions |
| `docs/usage/*.md` | ‚úÖ Yes | Keep in sync with workflow inputs |
| `examples/*/cicd.yml` | ‚úÖ Yes | Update when calling conventions change |
| `README.md` | ‚úÖ Yes | Keep inputs/outputs tables accurate |
| `CONTRIBUTING.md` | ‚úÖ Yes | |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | ‚ùå Never | Auto-generated by `wf-release.yml` |
| `.github/workflows/cicd.yml` | ‚öÝÔ∏è With care | This repo's own pipeline |
| `LICENSE` | ‚ùå Never | |
| `CODEOWNERS` | ‚ùå Never | |

When modifying a reusable workflow, always check and update if needed:
- `README.md` ‚Üí inputs/outputs table for that workflow
- `docs/usage/*.md` ‚Üí if the consumer calling convention changes
- `examples/*/cicd.yml` ‚Üí if new required inputs are added or existing ones renamed

---

## Permissions

Always define `permissions` at the **job level**, never at the workflow root level.
This applies to every workflow file ‚Äî reusable (`wf-*.yml`) or caller (`cicd.yml`, `lint.yml`, etc.).

```yaml
# Correct ‚Äî job level
jobs:
  build:
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

# Wrong ‚Äî root level
permissions:
  contents: read
jobs:
  build:
    ...
```

Assign only the minimum permissions required by each job (principle of least privilege).

---

## YAML formatting

- Indent: 2 spaces
- No `---` document start marker
- **Max line length: 180 characters** ‚Äî yamllint is configured with this limit and will fail the pipeline if any line exceeds it
- Long `run:` script lines must be split using shell line continuation (`\`) or by assigning intermediate variables
- Align values in `with:` blocks for readability:

```yaml
with:
  tag_version:       ${{ needs.setup.outputs.tag_version }}
  github_repository: ${{ github.repository }}
  author_name:       ${{ needs.setup.outputs.author_name }}
```

---

## GitHub Actions expression rules

- Never interpolate `${{ github.event.*.title }}` or other external values directly in `run:` scripts
- Always pass external values through `env:` blocks and reference as `$VARIABLE` in bash:

```yaml
# Correct
- name: Validate
  env:
    PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
  run: echo "$PR_TITLE"

# Wrong
- name: Validate
  run: echo "${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}"
```

---

## Secret handling pattern

Sensitive container env vars go in a single `APP_ENV_VARS` JSON secret:

```yaml
# In consumer cicd.yml ‚Äî correct pattern
secrets:
  APP_ENV_VARS: ${{ secrets.APP_ENV_VARS }}

# Inside wf-deploy.yml ‚Äî merged at deploy time via jq
```

Non-sensitive config goes in `vars.*` and can be interpolated in `with:` blocks.

- `GITHUB_TOKEN` must NEVER be declared in `workflow_call` secrets ‚Äî it is injected automatically
- `secrets.*` can ONLY be used in `secrets:` blocks, never inside `with:` inputs
- Optional secrets (e.g. `OPENAI_API_KEY`) must be declared with `required: false` in `workflow_call` and handled gracefully with `continue-on-error: true` so the pipeline never fails if they are absent
- AI-generated content uses `actions/ai-inference@v2` with `GITHUB_TOKEN` (requires `models: read` permission on the job) ‚Äî no external API keys needed

---

## Local vs remote workflow refs

This repo's own workflows use local refs:
```yaml
uses: ./.github/workflows/wf-setup.yml
```

Consumer repositories use remote refs:
```yaml
uses: francisjgarcia/actions-templates/.github/workflows/wf-setup.yml@main
```

Never use remote refs inside this repository's own workflow files.

---

## What not to do

- Do not push directly to `main` ‚Äî all changes go through a pull request
- Do not edit `CHANGELOG.md` directly ‚Äî auto-generated by `wf-release.yml`
- Do not add `on: push` or `on: schedule` to `wf-*.yml` files ‚Äî reusable only (`on: workflow_call`)
- Do not declare `GITHUB_TOKEN` in `workflow_call` secrets ‚Äî GitHub injects it automatically
- Do not use `secrets.*` inside `with:` input blocks
- Do not use `actions/checkout@main` or any `@latest` action ref ‚Äî always pin versions
- Do not hardcode usernames, org names, API tokens, or IP addresses
- Do not suggest adding `development` branch logic ‚Äî this repo uses Trunk Based Development
- Do not use `secrets: inherit` without explicit justification
- Do not add SSH deploy logic ‚Äî the runner deploys directly via docker context

---

## Checklist ‚Äî always verify before submitting

- [ ] Pinned version tags for all actions (`@v4`, `@v3.6.1`) ‚Äî never `@main` or `@latest`
- [ ] `env:` blocks used to pass external values into bash scripts
- [ ] `if: inputs.tag_version != ''` guard on every job in reusable workflows
- [ ] `retention-days: 1` on docker image artifacts
- [ ] Step summary (`$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY`) present on every job
- [ ] `permissions` defined at job level, not root level
- [ ] No `CHANGELOG.md` edits ‚Äî auto-generated
- [ ] No direct push to `main` ‚Äî always via pull request

---

## Language

- All code, YAML, variable names, comments, and documentation: **English**
- Conversation with the maintainer: **Spanish**
- Commit messages and PR titles: **English** (required by the semver automation)
